sentences.text: Standard privileged instructions to initialize, save, and restore extension state are provided to insulate privileged code from details of the added extension state by treating the state as an opaque object.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

sentences.text: The XS field provides a summary of all added extension state, but additional microarchitectural bits might be maintained in the extension to further reduce context save and restore overhead.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

sentences.text: The SD bit is read-only and is set when either the FS, VS, or XS bits encode a Dirty state (i.e., `SD=(FS0b11 OR XS0b11 OR VS0b11)`).
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

sentences.text: This allows privileged code to quickly determine when no additional context save is required beyond the integer register set and `pc`.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

sentences.text: The floating-point unit state is always initialized, saved, and restored using standard instructions (F, D, and/or Q), and privileged code must be aware of FLEN to determine the appropriate space to reserve for each `f` register.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

sentences.text: Machine and Supervisor modes share a single copy of the FS, VS, and XS bits.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

sentences.text: Supervisor-level software normally uses the FS, VS, and XS bits directly to record the status with respect to the supervisor-level saved context.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

sentences.text: Machine-level software must be more conservative in saving and restoring the extension state in their corresponding version of the context.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Extension Context Status in `mstatus` Register

sentences.text: The Zicfilp extension adds the `SPELP` and `MPELP` fields that hold the previous `ELP`, and are updated as specified in .
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Previous Expected Landing Pad (ELP) State in `mstatus` Register

sentences.text: The *x*`PELP` fields are encoded as follows:
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Previous Expected Landing Pad (ELP) State in `mstatus` Register

sentences.text: * 0 - `NOLPEXPECTED` - no landing pad instruction expected. * 1 - `LPEXPECTED` - a landing pad instruction is expected.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Status (`mstatus` and `mstatush`) Registers > Previous Expected Landing Pad (ELP) State in `mstatus` Register

sentences.text: The `mtvec` register is an MXLEN-bit *WARL* read/write register that holds trap vector configuration, consisting of a vector base address (BASE) and a vector mode (MODE).
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap-Vector Base-Address (`mtvec`) Register

sentences.text: .Encoding of mtvec MODE field. include::images/bytefield/mtvec.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap-Vector Base-Address (`mtvec`) Register

sentences.text: The `mtvec` register must always be implemented, but can contain a read-only value.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap-Vector Base-Address (`mtvec`) Register

sentences.text: If `mtvec` is writable, the set of values the register may hold can vary by implementation.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap-Vector Base-Address (`mtvec`) Register

sentences.text: The value in the BASE field must always be aligned on a 4-byte boundary, and the MODE setting may impose additional alignment constraints on the value in the BASE field.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap-Vector Base-Address (`mtvec`) Register

sentences.text: Note that the CSR contains only bits XLEN-1 through 2 of the address BASE.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap-Vector Base-Address (`mtvec`) Register

sentences.text: When used as an address, the lower two bits are filled with zeroes to obtain an XLEN-bit address that is always aligned on a 4-byte boundary.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap-Vector Base-Address (`mtvec`) Register

sentences.text: .Encoding of mtvec MODE field. | |Value |Name |Description |0 + 1 + {ge}2 |Direct + Vectored + --- |All traps set `pc` to BASE. + Asynchronous interrupts set `pc` to BASE+4{times}cause. + Reserved |
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap-Vector Base-Address (`mtvec`) Register

sentences.text: The encoding of the MODE field is shown in .
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap-Vector Base-Address (`mtvec`) Register

sentences.text: When MODE=Direct, all traps into machine mode cause the `pc` to be set to the address in the BASE field.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap-Vector Base-Address (`mtvec`) Register

sentences.text: When MODE=Vectored, all synchronous exceptions into machine mode cause the `pc` to be set to the address in the BASE field, whereas interrupts cause the `pc` to be set to the address in the BASE field plus four times the interrupt cause number.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap-Vector Base-Address (`mtvec`) Register

sentences.text: For example, a machine-mode timer interrupt (see ) causes the `pc` to be set to BASE+`0x1c`.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap-Vector Base-Address (`mtvec`) Register

sentences.text: An implementation may have different alignment constraints for different modes.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap-Vector Base-Address (`mtvec`) Register

sentences.text: In particular, MODE=Vectored may have stricter alignment constraints than MODE=Direct.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap-Vector Base-Address (`mtvec`) Register

sentences.text: By default, all traps at any privilege level are handled in machine mode, though a machine-mode handler can redirect traps back to the appropriate level with the MRET instruction ().
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Delegation (`medeleg` and `mideleg`) Registers

sentences.text: To increase performance, implementations can provide individual read/write bits within `medeleg` and `mideleg` to indicate that certain exceptions and interrupts should be processed directly by a lower privilege level.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Delegation (`medeleg` and `mideleg`) Registers

sentences.text: The machine exception delegation register (`medeleg`) is a 64-bit read/write register.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Delegation (`medeleg` and `mideleg`) Registers

sentences.text: The machine interrupt delegation (`mideleg`) register is an MXLEN-bit read/write register.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Delegation (`medeleg` and `mideleg`) Registers

sentences.text: In harts with S-mode, the `medeleg` and `mideleg` registers must exist, and setting a bit in `medeleg` or `mideleg` will delegate the corresponding trap, when occurring in S-mode or U-mode, to the S-mode trap handler.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Delegation (`medeleg` and `mideleg`) Registers

sentences.text: In harts without S-mode, the `medeleg` and `mideleg` registers should not exist.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Delegation (`medeleg` and `mideleg`) Registers

sentences.text: When a trap is delegated to S-mode, the `scause` register is written with the trap cause; the `sepc` register is written with the virtual address of the instruction that took the trap; the `stval` register is written with an exception-specific datum; the SPP field of `mstatus` is written with the active privilege mode at the time of the trap; the SPIE field of `mstatus` is written with the value of the SIE field at the time of the trap; and the SIE field of `mstatus` is cleared.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Delegation (`medeleg` and `mideleg`) Registers

sentences.text: The `mcause`, `mepc`, and `mtval` registers and the MPP and MPIE fields of `mstatus` are not written.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Delegation (`medeleg` and `mideleg`) Registers

sentences.text: An implementation can choose to subset the delegatable traps, with the supported delegatable bits found by writing one to every bit location, then reading back the value in `medeleg` or `mideleg` to see which bit positions hold a one.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Delegation (`medeleg` and `mideleg`) Registers

sentences.text: An implementation shall not have any bits of `medeleg` be read-only one, i.e., any synchronous trap that can be delegated must support not being delegated.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Delegation (`medeleg` and `mideleg`) Registers

sentences.text: Similarly, an implementation shall not fix as read-only one any bits of `mideleg` corresponding to machine-level interrupts (but may do so for lower-level interrupts).
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Delegation (`medeleg` and `mideleg`) Registers

sentences.text: Traps never transition from a more-privileged mode to a less-privileged mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Delegation (`medeleg` and `mideleg`) Registers

sentences.text: For example, if M-mode has delegated illegal-instruction exceptions to S-mode, and M-mode software later executes an illegal instruction, the trap is taken in M-mode, rather than being delegated to S-mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Delegation (`medeleg` and `mideleg`) Registers

sentences.text: By contrast, traps may be taken horizontally.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Delegation (`medeleg` and `mideleg`) Registers

sentences.text: Using the same example, if M-mode has delegated illegal-instruction exceptions to S-mode, and S-mode software later executes an illegal instruction, the trap is taken in S-mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Delegation (`medeleg` and `mideleg`) Registers

sentences.text: Delegated interrupts result in the interrupt being masked at the delegator privilege level.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Delegation (`medeleg` and `mideleg`) Registers

sentences.text: For example, if the supervisor timer interrupt (STI) is delegated to S-mode by setting `mideleg`[5], STIs will not be taken when executing in M-mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Delegation (`medeleg` and `mideleg`) Registers

sentences.text: By contrast, if `mideleg`[5] is clear, STIs can be taken in any mode and regardless of current mode will transfer control to M-mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Delegation (`medeleg` and `mideleg`) Registers

sentences.text: .Machine Exception Delegation (`medeleg`) register. include::images/bytefield/medeleg.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Delegation (`medeleg` and `mideleg`) Registers

sentences.text: `medeleg` has a bit position allocated for every synchronous exception shown in , with the index of the bit position equal to the value returned in the `mcause` register (i.e., setting bit 8 allows user-mode environment calls to be delegated to a lower-privilege trap handler).
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Delegation (`medeleg` and `mideleg`) Registers

sentences.text: When XLEN=32, `medelegh` is a 32-bit read/write register that aliases bits 63:32 of `medeleg`.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Delegation (`medeleg` and `mideleg`) Registers

sentences.text: The `medelegh` register does not exist when XLEN=64.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Delegation (`medeleg` and `mideleg`) Registers

sentences.text: .Machine Interrupt Delegation (`mideleg`) Register. include::images/bytefield/mideleg.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Delegation (`medeleg` and `mideleg`) Registers

sentences.text: `mideleg` holds trap delegation bits for individual interrupts, with the layout of bits matching those in the `mip` register (i.e., STIP interrupt delegation control is located in bit 5).
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Delegation (`medeleg` and `mideleg`) Registers

sentences.text: For exceptions that cannot occur in less privileged modes, the corresponding `medeleg` bits should be read-only zero.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Delegation (`medeleg` and `mideleg`) Registers

sentences.text: In particular, `medeleg`[11] is read-only zero.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Delegation (`medeleg` and `mideleg`) Registers

sentences.text: The `medeleg`[16] is read-only zero as double trap is not delegatable.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Delegation (`medeleg` and `mideleg`) Registers

sentences.text: The `mip` register is an MXLEN-bit read/write register containing information on pending interrupts, while `mie` is the corresponding MXLEN-bit read/write register containing interrupt enable bits.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Interrupt (`mip` and `mie`) Registers

sentences.text: Interrupt cause number i (as reported in CSR `mcause`, ) corresponds with bit i in both `mip` and `mie`.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Interrupt (`mip` and `mie`) Registers

sentences.text: Bits 15:0 are allocated to standard interrupt causes only, while bits 16 and above are designated for platform use.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Interrupt (`mip` and `mie`) Registers

sentences.text: NOTE: Interrupts designated for platform use may be designated for custom use at the platform's discretion.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Interrupt (`mip` and `mie`) Registers

sentences.text: .Machine Interrupt-Pending (`mip`) register. include::images/bytefield/mideleg.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Interrupt (`mip` and `mie`) Registers

sentences.text: .Machine Interrupt-Enable (`mie`) register include::images/bytefield/mideleg.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Interrupt (`mip` and `mie`) Registers

sentences.text: An interrupt i will trap to M-mode (causing the privilege mode to change to M-mode) if all of the following are true: (a) either the current privilege mode is M and the MIE bit in the `mstatus` register is set, or the current privilege mode has less privilege than M-mode; (b) bit i is set in both `mip` and `mie`; and (c) if register `mideleg` exists, bit i is not set in `mideleg`.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Interrupt (`mip` and `mie`) Registers

sentences.text: These conditions for an interrupt trap to occur must be evaluated in a bounded amount of time from when an interrupt becomes, or ceases to be, pending in `mip`, and must also be evaluated immediately following the execution of an xRET instruction or an explicit write to a CSR on which these interrupt trap conditions expressly depend (including `mip`, `mie`, `mstatus`, and `mideleg`).
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Interrupt (`mip` and `mie`) Registers

sentences.text: Interrupts to M-mode take priority over any interrupts to lower privilege modes.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Interrupt (`mip` and `mie`) Registers

sentences.text: Each individual bit in register `mip` may be writable or may be read-only.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Interrupt (`mip` and `mie`) Registers

sentences.text: When bit i in `mip` is writable, a pending interrupt i can be cleared by writing 0 to this bit.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Interrupt (`mip` and `mie`) Registers

sentences.text: If interrupt i can become pending but bit i in `mip` is read-only, the implementation must provide some other mechanism for clearing the pending interrupt.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Interrupt (`mip` and `mie`) Registers

sentences.text: A bit in `mie` must be writable if the corresponding interrupt can ever become pending.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Interrupt (`mip` and `mie`) Registers

sentences.text: Bits of `mie` that are not writable must be read-only zero.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Interrupt (`mip` and `mie`) Registers

sentences.text: The standard portions (bits 15:0) of the `mip` and `mie` registers are formatted as shown in and respectively.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Interrupt (`mip` and `mie`) Registers

sentences.text: .Standard portion (bits 15:0) of `mip`. include::images/bytefield/mipreg-standard.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Interrupt (`mip` and `mie`) Registers

sentences.text: .Standard portion (bits 15:0) of `mie`. include::images/bytefield/miereg-standard.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Interrupt (`mip` and `mie`) Registers

sentences.text: Bits `mip`.MEIP and `mie`.MEIE are the interrupt-pending and interrupt-enable bits for machine-level external interrupts. MEIP is read-only in `mip`, and is set and cleared by a platform-specific interrupt controller.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Interrupt (`mip` and `mie`) Registers

sentences.text: Bits `mip`.MTIP and `mie`.MTIE are the interrupt-pending and interrupt-enable bits for machine timer interrupts. MTIP is read-only in the `mip` register, and is cleared by writing to the memory-mapped machine-mode timer compare register.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Interrupt (`mip` and `mie`) Registers

sentences.text: Bits `mip`.MSIP and `mie`.MSIE are the interrupt-pending and interrupt-enable bits for machine-level software interrupts. MSIP is read-only in `mip`, and is written by accesses to memory-mapped control registers, which are used to provide machine-level interprocessor interrupts.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Interrupt (`mip` and `mie`) Registers

sentences.text: A hart's memory-mapped `msip` register is a 32-bit read/write register, where bits 31--1 read as zero and bit 0 contains the MSIP bit.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Interrupt (`mip` and `mie`) Registers

sentences.text: When the memory-mapped `msip` register changes, it is guaranteed to be reflected in `mip`.MSIP eventually, but not necessarily immediately.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Interrupt (`mip` and `mie`) Registers

sentences.text: If a system has only one hart, or if a platform standard supports the delivery of machine-level interprocessor interrupts through external interrupts (MEI) instead, then `mip`.MSIP and `mie`.MSIE may both be read-only zeros.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Interrupt (`mip` and `mie`) Registers

sentences.text: If supervisor mode is not implemented, bits SEIP, STIP, and SSIP of `mip` and SEIE, STIE, and SSIE of `mie` are read-only zeros.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Interrupt (`mip` and `mie`) Registers

sentences.text: If supervisor mode is implemented, bits `mip`.SEIP and `mie`.SEIE are the interrupt-pending and interrupt-enable bits for supervisor-level external interrupts. SEIP is writable in `mip`, and may be written by M-mode software to indicate to S-mode that an external interrupt is pending.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Interrupt (`mip` and `mie`) Registers

sentences.text: Additionally, the platform-level interrupt controller may generate supervisor-level external interrupts.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Interrupt (`mip` and `mie`) Registers

sentences.text: Supervisor-level external interrupts are made pending based on the logical-OR of the software-writable SEIP bit and the signal from the external interrupt controller.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Interrupt (`mip` and `mie`) Registers

sentences.text: When `mip` is read with a CSR instruction, the value of the SEIP bit returned in the `rd` destination register is the logical-OR of the software-writable bit and the interrupt signal from the interrupt controller, but the signal from the interrupt controller is not used to calculate the value written to SEIP.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Interrupt (`mip` and `mie`) Registers

sentences.text: Only the software-writable SEIP bit participates in the read-modify-write sequence of a CSRRS or CSRRC instruction.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Interrupt (`mip` and `mie`) Registers

sentences.text: If supervisor mode is implemented, its `mip`.STIP and `mie`.STIE are the interrupt-pending and interrupt-enable bits for supervisor-level timer interrupts.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Interrupt (`mip` and `mie`) Registers

sentences.text: If the stimecmp register is not implemented, STIP is writable in mip, and may be written by M-mode software to deliver timer interrupts to S-mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Interrupt (`mip` and `mie`) Registers

sentences.text: If the `stimecmp` (supervisor-mode timer compare) register is implemented, STIP is read-only in mip and reflects the supervisor-level timer interrupt signal resulting from stimecmp.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Interrupt (`mip` and `mie`) Registers

sentences.text: This timer interrupt signal is cleared by writing `stimecmp` with a value greater than the current time value.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Interrupt (`mip` and `mie`) Registers

sentences.text: If supervisor mode is implemented, bits `mip`.SSIP and `mie`.SSIE are the interrupt-pending and interrupt-enable bits for supervisor-level software interrupts. SSIP is writable in `mip` and may also be set to 1 by a platform-specific interrupt controller.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Interrupt (`mip` and `mie`) Registers

sentences.text: If the Sscofpmf extension is implemented, bits `mip`.LCOFIP and `mie`.LCOFIE are the interrupt-pending and interrupt-enable bits for local-counter-overflow interrupts. LCOFIP is read-write in `mip` and reflects the occurrence of a local counter-overflow overflow interrupt request resulting from any of the `mhpmeventn`.OF bits being set.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Interrupt (`mip` and `mie`) Registers

sentences.text: If the Sscofpmf extension is not implemented, `mip`.LCOFIP and `mie`.LCOFIE are read-only zeros.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Interrupt (`mip` and `mie`) Registers

sentences.text: Multiple simultaneous interrupts destined for M-mode are handled in the following decreasing priority order: MEI, MSI, MTI, SEI, SSI, STI, LCOFI.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Interrupt (`mip` and `mie`) Registers

sentences.text: Restricted views of the `mip` and `mie` registers appear as the `sip` and `sie` registers for supervisor level.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Interrupt (`mip` and `mie`) Registers

sentences.text: If an interrupt is delegated to S-mode by setting a bit in the `mideleg` register, it becomes visible in the `sip` register and is maskable using the `sie` register.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Interrupt (`mip` and `mie`) Registers

sentences.text: Otherwise, the corresponding bits in `sip` and `sie` are read-only zero.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Interrupt (`mip` and `mie`) Registers

sentences.text: M-mode includes a basic hardware performance-monitoring facility.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Hardware Performance Monitor

sentences.text: The `mcycle` CSR counts the number of clock cycles executed by the processor core on which the hart is running.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Hardware Performance Monitor

sentences.text: The `minstret` CSR counts the number of instructions the hart has retired.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Hardware Performance Monitor

sentences.text: The `mcycle` and `minstret` registers have 64-bit precision on all RV32 and RV64 harts.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Hardware Performance Monitor

sentences.text: The counter registers have an arbitrary value after the hart is reset, and can be written with a given value.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Hardware Performance Monitor

sentences.text: Any CSR write takes effect after the writing instruction has otherwise completed.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Hardware Performance Monitor

sentences.text: The `mcycle` CSR may be shared between harts on the same core, in which case writes to `mcycle` will be visible to those harts.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Hardware Performance Monitor

sentences.text: The platform should provide a mechanism to indicate which harts share an `mcycle` CSR.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Hardware Performance Monitor

sentences.text: The hardware performance monitor includes 29 additional 64-bit event counters, `mhpmcounter3`-`mhpmcounter31`.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Hardware Performance Monitor

sentences.text: The event selector CSRs, `mhpmevent3`-`mhpmevent31`, are 64-bit *WARL* registers that control which event causes the corresponding counter to increment.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Hardware Performance Monitor

sentences.text: The meaning of these events is defined by the platform, but event 0 is defined to mean "no event." All counters should be implemented, but a legal implementation is to make both the counter and its corresponding event selector be read-only 0.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Hardware Performance Monitor

sentences.text: .Hardware performance monitor counters. include::images/bytefield/hpmevents.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Hardware Performance Monitor

sentences.text: The `mhpmcounters` are *WARL* registers that support up to 64 bits of precision on RV32 and RV64.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Hardware Performance Monitor

sentences.text: When XLEN=32, reads of the `mcycle`, `minstret`, `mhpmcountern`, and `mhpmeventn` CSRs return bitj 31-0 of the corresponding register, and writes change only bits 31-0; reads of the `mcycleh`, `minstreth`, `mhpmcounternh`, and `mhpmeventnh` CSRs return bits 63-32 of the corresponding register, and writes change only bits 63-32.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Hardware Performance Monitor

sentences.text: The `mhpmeventnh` CSRs are provided only if the Sscofpmf extension is implemented.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Hardware Performance Monitor

sentences.text: The counter-enable `mcounteren` register is a 32-bit register that controls the availability of the hardware performance-monitoring counters to the next-lower privileged mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Counter-Enable (`mcounteren`) Register

sentences.text: .Counter-enable (`mcounteren`) register. include::images/bytefield/counteren.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Counter-Enable (`mcounteren`) Register

sentences.text: The settings in this register only control accessibility.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Counter-Enable (`mcounteren`) Register

sentences.text: The act of reading or writing this register does not affect the underlying counters, which continue to increment even when not accessible.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Counter-Enable (`mcounteren`) Register

sentences.text: When the CY, TM, IR, or HPMn bit in the `mcounteren` register is clear, attempts to read the `cycle`, `time`, `instret`, or `hpmcountern` register while executing in S-mode or U-mode will cause an illegal-instruction exception.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Counter-Enable (`mcounteren`) Register

sentences.text: When one of these bits is set, access to the corresponding register is permitted in the next implemented privilege mode (S-mode if implemented, otherwise U-mode).
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Counter-Enable (`mcounteren`) Register

sentences.text: In addition, when the TM bit in the `mcounteren` register is clear, attempts to access the `stimecmp` or `vstimecmp` register while executing in a mode less privileged than M will cause an illegal-instruction exception.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Counter-Enable (`mcounteren`) Register

sentences.text: When this bit is set, access to the `stimecmp` or `vstimecmp` register is permitted in S-mode if implemented, and access to the `vstimecmp` register (via `stimecmp`) is permitted in VS-mode if implemented and not otherwise prevented by the TM bit in `hcounteren`.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Counter-Enable (`mcounteren`) Register

sentences.text: The `cycle`, `instret`, and `hpmcountern` CSRs are read-only shadows of `mcycle`, `minstret`, and `mhpmcounter n`, respectively.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Counter-Enable (`mcounteren`) Register

sentences.text: The `time` CSR is a read-only shadow of the memory-mapped `mtime` register.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Counter-Enable (`mcounteren`) Register

sentences.text: Analogously, when XLEN=32, the `cycleh`, `instreth` and `hpmcounternh` CSRs are read-only shadows of `mcycleh`, `minstreth` and `mhpmcounternh`, respectively.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Counter-Enable (`mcounteren`) Register

sentences.text: When XLEN=32, the `timeh` CSR is a read-only shadow of the upper 32 bits of the memory-mapped `mtime` register, while `time` shadows only the lower 32 bits of `mtime`.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Counter-Enable (`mcounteren`) Register

sentences.text: In harts with U-mode, the `mcounteren` must be implemented, but all fields are *WARL* and may be read-only zero, indicating reads to the corresponding counter will cause an illegal-instruction exception when executing in a less-privileged mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Counter-Enable (`mcounteren`) Register

sentences.text: In harts without U-mode, the `mcounteren` register should not exist.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Counter-Enable (`mcounteren`) Register

sentences.text: .Counter-inhibit `mcountinhibit` register include::images/bytefield/counterinh.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Counter-Inhibit (`mcountinhibit`) Register

sentences.text: The counter-inhibit register `mcountinhibit` is a 32-bit *WARL* register that controls which of the hardware performance-monitoring counters increment.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Counter-Inhibit (`mcountinhibit`) Register

sentences.text: The settings in this register only control whether the counters increment; their accessibility is not affected by the setting of this register.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Counter-Inhibit (`mcountinhibit`) Register

sentences.text: When the CY, IR, or HPMn bit in the `mcountinhibit` register is clear, the `mcycle`, `minstret`, or `mhpmcountern` register increments as usual.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Counter-Inhibit (`mcountinhibit`) Register

sentences.text: When the CY, IR, or HPMn bit is set, the corresponding counter does not increment.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Counter-Inhibit (`mcountinhibit`) Register

sentences.text: The `mcycle` CSR may be shared between harts on the same core, in which case the `mcountinhibit.CY` field is also shared between those harts, and so writes to `mcountinhibit.CY` will be visible to those harts.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Counter-Inhibit (`mcountinhibit`) Register

sentences.text: If the `mcountinhibit` register is not implemented, the implementation behaves as though the register were set to zero.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Counter-Inhibit (`mcountinhibit`) Register

sentences.text: The `mscratch` register is an MXLEN-bit read/write register dedicated for use by machine mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Scratch (`mscratch`) Register

sentences.text: Typically, it is used to hold a pointer to a machine-mode hart-local context space and swapped with a user register upon entry to an M-mode trap handler.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Scratch (`mscratch`) Register

sentences.text: .Machine-mode scratch register. include::images/bytefield/mscratch.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Scratch (`mscratch`) Register

sentences.text: `mepc` is an MXLEN-bit read/write register formatted as shown in .
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Exception Program Counter (`mepc`) Register

sentences.text: The low bit of `mepc` (`mepc[0]`) is always zero.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Exception Program Counter (`mepc`) Register

sentences.text: On implementations that support only IALIGN=32, the two low bits (`mepc[1:0]`) are always zero.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Exception Program Counter (`mepc`) Register

sentences.text: If an implementation allows IALIGN to be either 16 or 32 (by changing CSR `misa`, for example), then, whenever IALIGN=32, bit `mepc[1]` is masked on reads so that it appears to be 0.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Exception Program Counter (`mepc`) Register

sentences.text: This masking occurs also for the implicit read by the MRET instruction.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Exception Program Counter (`mepc`) Register

sentences.text: Though masked, `mepc[1]` remains writable when IALIGN=32.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Exception Program Counter (`mepc`) Register

sentences.text: `mepc` is a *WARL* register that must be able to hold all valid virtual addresses.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Exception Program Counter (`mepc`) Register

sentences.text: It need not be capable of holding all possible invalid addresses.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Exception Program Counter (`mepc`) Register

sentences.text: Prior to writing `mepc`, implementations may convert an invalid address into some other invalid address that `mepc` is capable of holding.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Exception Program Counter (`mepc`) Register

sentences.text: When a trap is taken into M-mode, `mepc` is written with the virtual address of the instruction that was interrupted or that encountered the exception.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Exception Program Counter (`mepc`) Register

sentences.text: Otherwise, `mepc` is never written by the implementation, though it may be explicitly written by software.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Exception Program Counter (`mepc`) Register

sentences.text: .Machine exception program counter register. include::images/bytefield/mepcreg.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Exception Program Counter (`mepc`) Register

sentences.text: The `mcause` register is an MXLEN-bit read-write register formatted as shown in .
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Cause (`mcause`) Register

sentences.text: When a trap is taken into M-mode, `mcause` is written with a code indicating the event that caused the trap.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Cause (`mcause`) Register

sentences.text: Otherwise, `mcause` is never written by the implementation, though it may be explicitly written by software.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Cause (`mcause`) Register

sentences.text: The Interrupt bit in the `mcause` register is set if the trap was caused by an interrupt.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Cause (`mcause`) Register

sentences.text: The Exception Code field contains a code identifying the last exception or interrupt. lists the possible machine-level exception codes.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Cause (`mcause`) Register

sentences.text: The Exception Code is a *WLRL* field, so is only guaranteed to hold supported exception codes.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Cause (`mcause`) Register

sentences.text: .Machine Cause (`mcause`) register. include::images/bytefield/mcausereg.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Cause (`mcause`) Register

sentences.text: Note that load and load-reserved instructions generate load exceptions, whereas store, store-conditional, and AMO instructions generate store/AMO exceptions.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Cause (`mcause`) Register

sentences.text: If an instruction may raise multiple synchronous exceptions, the decreasing priority order of indicates which exception is taken and reported in `mcause`.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Cause (`mcause`) Register

sentences.text: The priority of any custom synchronous exceptions is implementation-defined.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Cause (`mcause`) Register

sentences.text: When a virtual address is translated into a physical address, the address translation algorithm determines what specific exception may be raised.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Cause (`mcause`) Register

sentences.text: Load/store/AMO address-misaligned exceptions may have either higher or lower priority than load/store/AMO page-fault and access-fault exceptions.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Cause (`mcause`) Register

sentences.text: The `mtval` register is an MXLEN-bit read-write register formatted as shown in .
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Value (`mtval`) Register

sentences.text: When a trap is taken into M-mode, `mtval` is either set to zero or written with exception-specific information to assist software in handling the trap.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Value (`mtval`) Register

sentences.text: Otherwise, `mtval` is never written by the implementation, though it may be explicitly written by software.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Value (`mtval`) Register

sentences.text: The hardware platform will specify which exceptions must set `mtval` informatively, which may unconditionally set it to zero, and which may exhibit either behavior, depending on the underlying event that caused the exception.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Value (`mtval`) Register

sentences.text: If the hardware platform specifies that no exceptions set `mtval` to a nonzero value, then `mtval` is read-only zero.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Value (`mtval`) Register

sentences.text: If `mtval` is written with a nonzero value when a breakpoint, address-misaligned, access-fault, page-fault, or hardware-error exception occurs on an instruction fetch, load, or store, then `mtval` will contain the faulting virtual address.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Value (`mtval`) Register

sentences.text: On a breakpoint exception raised by an EBREAK or C.EBREAK instruction, `mtval` is written with either zero or the virtual address of the instruction.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Value (`mtval`) Register

sentences.text: NOTE: For breakpoint exceptions raised by [C.]EBREAK, the virtual address of the instruction is already recorded in `mepc`.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Value (`mtval`) Register

sentences.text: Recording the same address in `mtval` is redundant; the option is provided for backwards compatibility.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Value (`mtval`) Register

sentences.text: When page-based virtual memory is enabled, `mtval` is written with the faulting virtual address, even for physical-memory access-fault exceptions.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Value (`mtval`) Register

sentences.text: This design reduces datapath cost for most implementations, particularly those with hardware page-table walkers.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Value (`mtval`) Register

sentences.text: .Machine Trap Value (`mtval`) register. include::images/bytefield/mtvalreg.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Value (`mtval`) Register

sentences.text: If `mtval` is written with a nonzero value when a misaligned load or store causes an access-fault, page-fault, or hardware-error exception, then `mtval` will contain the virtual address of the portion of the access that caused the fault.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Value (`mtval`) Register

sentences.text: If `mtval` is written with a nonzero value when an instruction access-fault, page-fault, or hardware-error exception occurs on a hart with variable-length instructions, then `mtval` will contain the virtual address of the portion of the instruction that caused the fault, while `mepc` will point to the beginning of the instruction.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Value (`mtval`) Register

sentences.text: The `mtval` register can optionally also be used to return the faulting instruction bits on an illegal-instruction exception (`mepc` points to the faulting instruction in memory).
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Value (`mtval`) Register

sentences.text: If `mtval` is written with a nonzero value when an illegal-instruction exception occurs, then `mtval` will contain the shortest of:
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Value (`mtval`) Register

sentences.text: * the actual faulting instruction
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Value (`mtval`) Register

sentences.text: the first ILEN bits of the faulting instruction
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Value (`mtval`) Register

sentences.text: the first MXLEN bits of the faulting instruction
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Value (`mtval`) Register

sentences.text: The value loaded into `mtval` on an illegal-instruction exception is right-justified and all unused upper bits are cleared to zero.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Value (`mtval`) Register

sentences.text: On a trap caused by a software-check exception, the `mtval` register holds the cause for the exception.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Value (`mtval`) Register

sentences.text: The following encodings are defined:
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Value (`mtval`) Register

sentences.text: * 0 - No information provided. * 2 - Landing Pad Fault.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Value (`mtval`) Register

sentences.text: Defined by the Zicfilp extension (). * 3 - Shadow Stack Fault.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Value (`mtval`) Register

sentences.text: Defined by the Zicfiss extension ().
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Value (`mtval`) Register

sentences.text: For other traps, `mtval` is set to zero, but a future standard may redefine `mtval`’s setting for other traps.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Value (`mtval`) Register

sentences.text: If `mtval` is not read-only zero, it is a *WARL* register that must be able to hold all valid virtual addresses and the value zero.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Value (`mtval`) Register

sentences.text: It need not be capable of holding all possible invalid addresses.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Value (`mtval`) Register

sentences.text: Prior to writing `mtval`, implementations may convert an invalid address into some other invalid address that `mtval` is capable of holding.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Value (`mtval`) Register

sentences.text: If the feature to return the faulting instruction bits is implemented, `mtval` must also be able to hold all values less than 2^N^, where N is the smaller of MXLEN and ILEN.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Trap Value (`mtval`) Register

sentences.text: The `mconfigptr` register is an MXLEN-bit read-only CSR formatted as shown in , that holds the physical address of a configuration data structure.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Configuration Pointer (`mconfigptr`) Register

sentences.text: Software can traverse this data structure to discover information about the harts, the platform, and their configuration.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Configuration Pointer (`mconfigptr`) Register

sentences.text: .Machine Configuration Pointer (`mconfigptr`) register. include::images/bytefield/mconfigptrreg.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Configuration Pointer (`mconfigptr`) Register

sentences.text: The pointer alignment in bits must be no smaller than MXLEN: i.e., if MXLEN is 8{times}n, then `mconfigptr`[log~2n~-1:0] must be zero.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Configuration Pointer (`mconfigptr`) Register

sentences.text: The `mconfigptr` register must be implemented, but it may be zero to indicate the configuration data structure does not exist or that an alternative mechanism must be used to locate it.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Configuration Pointer (`mconfigptr`) Register

sentences.text: The `menvcfg` CSR is a 64-bit read/write register, formatted as shown in , that controls certain characteristics of the execution environment for modes less privileged than M.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Environment Configuration (`menvcfg`) Register

sentences.text: .Machine environment configuration (`menvcfg`) register. include::images/wavedrom/menvcfgreg.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Environment Configuration (`menvcfg`) Register

sentences.text: If bit FIOM (Fence of I/O implies Memory) is set to one in `menvcfg`, FENCE instructions executed in modes less privileged than M are modified so the requirement to order accesses to device I/O implies also the requirement to order main memory accesses. details the modified interpretation of FENCE instruction bits PI, PO, SI, and SO for modes less privileged than M when FIOM=1.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Environment Configuration (`menvcfg`) Register

sentences.text: Similarly, for modes less privileged than M when FIOM=1, if an atomic instruction that accesses a region ordered as device I/O has its aq and/or rl bit set, then that instruction is ordered as though it accesses both device I/O and memory.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Environment Configuration (`menvcfg`) Register

sentences.text: If S-mode is not supported, or if `satp`.MODE is read-only zero (always Bare), the implementation may make FIOM read-only zero.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Environment Configuration (`menvcfg`) Register

sentences.text: .Modified interpretation of FENCE predecessor and successor sets for modes less privileged than M when FIOM=1. | |Instruction bit |Meaning when set |PI + PO |Predecessor device input and memory reads (PR implied) + Predecessor device output and memory writes (PW implied) |SI + SO |Successor device input and memory reads (SR implied) + Successor device output and memory writes (SW implied) |
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Environment Configuration (`menvcfg`) Register

sentences.text: The PBMTE bit controls whether the Svpbmt extension is available for use in S-mode and G-stage address translation (i.e., for page tables pointed to by `satp` or `hgatp`).
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Environment Configuration (`menvcfg`) Register

sentences.text: When PBMTE=1, Svpbmt is available for S-mode and G-stage address translation.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Environment Configuration (`menvcfg`) Register

sentences.text: When PBMTE=0, the implementation behaves as though Svpbmt were not implemented.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Environment Configuration (`menvcfg`) Register

sentences.text: If Svpbmt is not implemented, PBMTE is read-only zero.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Environment Configuration (`menvcfg`) Register

sentences.text: Furthermore, for implementations with the hypervisor extension, `henvcfg`.PBMTE is read-only zero if `menvcfg`.PBMTE is zero.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Environment Configuration (`menvcfg`) Register

sentences.text: After changing `menvcfg`.PBMTE, executing an SFENCE.VMA instruction with rs1=`x0` and rs2=`x0` suffices to synchronize address-translation caches with respect to the altered interpretation of page-table entries' PBMT fields.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Environment Configuration (`menvcfg`) Register

sentences.text: See for additional synchronization requirements when the hypervisor extension is implemented.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Environment Configuration (`menvcfg`) Register

sentences.text: If the Svadu extension is implemented, the ADUE bit controls whether hardware updating of PTE A/D bits is enabled for S-mode and G-stage address translations.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Environment Configuration (`menvcfg`) Register

sentences.text: When ADUE=1, hardware updating of PTE A/D bits is enabled during S-mode address translation, and the implementation behaves as though the Svade extension were not implemented for S-mode address translation.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Environment Configuration (`menvcfg`) Register

sentences.text: When the hypervisor extension is implemented, if ADUE=1, hardware updating of PTE A/D bits is enabled during G-stage address translation, and the implementation behaves as though the Svade extension were not implemented for G-stage address translation.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Environment Configuration (`menvcfg`) Register

sentences.text: When ADUE=0, the implementation behaves as though Svade were implemented for S-mode and G-stage address translation.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Environment Configuration (`menvcfg`) Register

sentences.text: If Svadu is not implemented, ADUE is read-only zero.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Environment Configuration (`menvcfg`) Register

sentences.text: Furthermore, for implementations with the hypervisor extension, `henvcfg`.ADUE is read-only zero if `menvcfg`.ADUE is zero.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Environment Configuration (`menvcfg`) Register

sentences.text: After changing `menvcfg`.ADUE, executing an SFENCE.VMA instruction with rs1=`x0` and rs2=`x0` suffices to synchronize address-translation caches with respect to the altered interpretation of page-table entries' A/D bits.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Environment Configuration (`menvcfg`) Register

sentences.text: See for additional synchronization requirements when the hypervisor extension is implemented.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Environment Configuration (`menvcfg`) Register

sentences.text: NOTE: The Svade extension requires page-fault exceptions be raised when PTE A/D bits need be set, hence Svade is implemented when ADUE=0.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Environment Configuration (`menvcfg`) Register

sentences.text: If the Smcdeleg extension is implemented, the CDE (Counter Delegation Enable) bit controls whether Zicntr and Zihpm counters can be delegated to S-mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Environment Configuration (`menvcfg`) Register

sentences.text: When CDE=1, the Smcdeleg extension is enabled, see .
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Environment Configuration (`menvcfg`) Register

sentences.text: When CDE=0, the Smcdeleg and Ssccfg extensions appear to be not implemented.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Environment Configuration (`menvcfg`) Register

sentences.text: If Smcdeleg is not implemented, CDE is read-only zero.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Environment Configuration (`menvcfg`) Register

sentences.text: The Sstc extension adds the `STCE` (STimecmp Enable) bit to `menvcfg` CSR.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Environment Configuration (`menvcfg`) Register

sentences.text: When the Sstc extension is not implemented, `STCE` is read-only zero.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Environment Configuration (`menvcfg`) Register

sentences.text: The `STCE` bit enables `stimecmp` for S-mode when set to one.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Environment Configuration (`menvcfg`) Register

sentences.text: When this extension is implemented and `STCE` in `menvcfg` is zero, an attempt to access `stimecmp` in a mode other than M-mode raises an illegal-instruction exception, `STCE` in `henvcfg` is read-only zero, and `STIP` in `mip` and `sip` reverts to its defined behavior as if this extension is not implemented.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Environment Configuration (`menvcfg`) Register

sentences.text: Further, if the H extension is implemented, then `hip`.VSTIP also reverts its defined behavior as if this extension is not implemented.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Environment Configuration (`menvcfg`) Register

sentences.text: The Zicboz extension adds the `CBZE` (Cache Block Zero instruction enable) field to `menvcfg`.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Environment Configuration (`menvcfg`) Register

sentences.text: When the `CBZE` field is set to 1, it enables execution of the cache block zero instruction, `CBO.ZERO`, in modes less privileged than M.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Environment Configuration (`menvcfg`) Register

sentences.text: Otherwise, the instruction raises an illegal-instruction exception in modes less privileged than M.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Environment Configuration (`menvcfg`) Register

sentences.text: When the Zicboz extension is not implemented, `CBZE` is read-only zero.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Environment Configuration (`menvcfg`) Register

sentences.text: The Zicbom extension adds the `CBCFE` (Cache Block Clean and Flush instruction Enable) field to `menvcfg`.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Environment Configuration (`menvcfg`) Register

sentences.text: When the `CBCFE` field is set to 1, it enables execution of the cache block clean instruction (`CBO.CLEAN`) and the cache block flush instruction (`CBO.FLUSH`) in modes less privileged than M.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Environment Configuration (`menvcfg`) Register

sentences.text: Otherwise, these instructions raise an illegal-instruction exception in modes less privileged than M.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Environment Configuration (`menvcfg`) Register

sentences.text: When the Zicbom extension is not implemented, `CBCFE` is read-only zero.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Environment Configuration (`menvcfg`) Register

sentences.text: The Zicbom extension adds the `CBIE` (Cache Block Invalidate instruction Enable) WARL field to `menvcfg` to control execution of the cache block invalidate instruction (`CBO.INVAL`) in modes less privileged than M.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Environment Configuration (`menvcfg`) Register

sentences.text: When `CBIE` is set to `00b`, the instruction raises an illegal-instruction exception in modes less privileged than M.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Environment Configuration (`menvcfg`) Register

sentences.text: When the Zicbom extension is not implemented, `CBIE` is read-only zero.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Environment Configuration (`menvcfg`) Register

sentences.text: The encoding `10b` is reserved.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Environment Configuration (`menvcfg`) Register

sentences.text: When `CBIE` is set to `01b` or `11b`, and when enabled for execution in modes less privileged than M, it behaves as follows:
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Environment Configuration (`menvcfg`) Register

sentences.text: * `01b` -- The instruction is executed and performs a flush operation, even if configured by a mode less privileged than M to perform an invalidate operation. * `11b` -- The instruction is executed and performs an invalidate operation, unless configured by a mode less privileged than M to perform a flush operation.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Environment Configuration (`menvcfg`) Register

sentences.text: When the `LPE` field is 0, the Zicfilp extension is not enabled in S-mode, and the following rules apply to S-mode.

sentences.text: When `SSE` field is 0, the following rules apply to privilege modes that are less than M:

sentences.text: * 32-bit Zicfiss instructions will revert to their behavior as defined by Zimop. * 16-bit Zicfiss instructions will revert to their behavior as defined by Zcmop. * The `pte.xwr=010b` encoding in VS/S-stage page tables becomes reserved. * `SSAMOSWAP.W/D` raises an illegal-instruction exception.

sentences.text: It exists if any extension that adds a field to `mseccfg` is implemented.

sentences.text: Otherwise, it is reserved.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Security Configuration (`mseccfg`) Register

sentences.text: The Zkr extension adds the `SSEED` and `USEED` fields to the `mseccfg` CSR to control access to the `seed` CSR from modes less privileged than M.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Security Configuration (`mseccfg`) Register

sentences.text: When `USEED` is 0, access to the `seed` CSR in U-mode raises an illegal-instruction exception.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Security Configuration (`mseccfg`) Register

sentences.text: When `USEED` is 1, read-write access to the `seed` CSR from U-mode is allowed; all other types of accesses raise an illegal-instruction exception.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Security Configuration (`mseccfg`) Register

sentences.text: If Zkr or U-mode is not implemented, `USEED` is read-only zero.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Security Configuration (`mseccfg`) Register

sentences.text: When `SSEED` is 0, access to the `seed` CSR from S-/HS-mode raises an illegal-instruction exception.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Security Configuration (`mseccfg`) Register

sentences.text: When `SSEED` is 1, read-write access to the `seed` CSR from S-/HS-mode is allowed; all other types of accesses raise an illegal-instruction exception.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Security Configuration (`mseccfg`) Register

sentences.text: If Zkr or S-mode is not implemented, `SSEED` is read-only zero.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Security Configuration (`mseccfg`) Register

sentences.text: When the H extension is also implemented, access to the `seed` CSR from an HS-qualified instruction leads to a virtual-instruction exception in VS and VU modes; all other types of accesses raise an illegal-instruction exception.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Security Configuration (`mseccfg`) Register

sentences.text: .Entropy Source Access Control. [cols="1,1,1,7",options="header",] | |Mode | `SSEED` | `USEED` | Description | M | - | - | The `seed` CSR is always available in machine mode as normal (with a CSR read-write instruction.) Attempted read without a write raises an illegal-instruction exception regardless of mode and access control bits. | U | - | `0` | Any `seed` CSR access raises an illegal-instruction exception. | U | - | `1` | The `seed` CSR is accessible as normal.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Security Configuration (`mseccfg`) Register

sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Security Configuration (`mseccfg`) Register

sentences.text: The Smepmp extension adds the `RLB`, `MMWP`, and the `MML` fields in `mseccfg`.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Security Configuration (`mseccfg`) Register

sentences.text: When `mseccfg.RLB` (Rule Locking Bypass) a WARL field that provides a mechanism to temporarily modify *Locked* PMP rules.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Security Configuration (`mseccfg`) Register

sentences.text: When `mseccfg.RLB` is 1, locked PMP rules may be removed or modified and locked PMP rules may be edited.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Security Configuration (`mseccfg`) Register

sentences.text: When `mseccfg.RLB` is 0 and `pmpcfg.L` is 1 in any rule or entry (including disabled entries), then `mseccfg.RLB` remains 0 and any further modifications to `mseccfg.RLB` are ignored until a *PMP reset*.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Security Configuration (`mseccfg`) Register

sentences.text: The `mseccfg.MMWP` (Machine-Mode Allowlist Policy) is a WARL field.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Security Configuration (`mseccfg`) Register

sentences.text: This field changes the default PMP policy for Machine mode when accessing memory regions that don't have a matching PMP rule.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Security Configuration (`mseccfg`) Register

sentences.text: This is a sticky bit, meaning that once set it cannot be unset until a *PMP reset*.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Security Configuration (`mseccfg`) Register

sentences.text: When set it changes the default PMP policy for M-mode when accessing memory regions that don’t have a matching *PMP rule*, to *denied* instead of *ignored*.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Security Configuration (`mseccfg`) Register

sentences.text: The `mseccfg.MML` (Machine Mode Lockdown) is a WARL field.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Security Configuration (`mseccfg`) Register

sentences.text: The `MML` bit changes the interpretation of the `pmpcfg.L` bit defined in .
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Security Configuration (`mseccfg`) Register

sentences.text: This is a sticky bit, meaning that once set it cannot be unset until a *PMP reset*.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Security Configuration (`mseccfg`) Register

sentences.text: When `mseccfg.MML` is set the system's behavior changes in the following way:
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Security Configuration (`mseccfg`) Register

sentences.text: The meaning of `pmpcfg.L` changes: Instead of marking a rule as *locked* and *enforced* in all modes, it now marks a rule as *M-mode-only* when set and *S/U-mode-only* when unset.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Security Configuration (`mseccfg`) Register

sentences.text: The formerly reserved encoding of `pmpcfg.RW=01`, and the encoding `pmpcfg.LRWX=1111`, now encode a *Shared-Region*. + + An M-mode-only rule is *enforced* on Machine mode and *denied* in Supervisor or User mode.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Security Configuration (`mseccfg`) Register

sentences.text: It also remains *locked* so that any further modifications to its associated configuration or address registers are ignored until a *PMP reset*, unless `mseccfg.RLB` is set. + + An S/U-mode-only rule is *enforced* on Supervisor and User modes and *denied* on Machine mode. + + A Shared-Region rule is *enforced* on all modes, with restrictions depending on the `pmpcfg.L` and `pmpcfg.X` bits: + +
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Security Configuration (`mseccfg`) Register

sentences.text: A Shared-Region rule where `pmpcfg.L` is not set can be used for sharing data between M-mode and S/U-mode, so is not executable. M-mode has read/write access to that region, and S/U-mode has read access if `pmpcfg.X` is not set, or read/write access if `pmpcfg.X` is set. + * A Shared-Region rule where `pmpcfg.L` is set can be used for sharing code between M-mode and S/U-mode, so is not writable.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Security Configuration (`mseccfg`) Register

sentences.text: Both M-mode and S/U-mode have execute access on the region, and M-mode also has read access if `pmpcfg.X` is set.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Security Configuration (`mseccfg`) Register

sentences.text: The rule remains *locked* so that any further modifications to its associated configuration or address registers are ignored until a *PMP reset*, unless `mseccfg.RLB` is set. + * The encoding `pmpcfg.LRWX=1111` can be used for sharing data between M-mode and S/U mode, where both modes only have read-only access to the region.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Security Configuration (`mseccfg`) Register

sentences.text: The rule remains *locked* so that any further modifications to its associated configuration or address registers are ignored until a *PMP reset*, unless `mseccfg.RLB` is set.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Security Configuration (`mseccfg`) Register

sentences.text: Adding a rule with executable privileges that either is *M-mode-only* or a *locked* *Shared-Region* is not possible and such `pmpcfg` writes are ignored, leaving `pmpcfg` unchanged.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Security Configuration (`mseccfg`) Register

sentences.text: This restriction can be temporarily lifted by setting `mseccfg.RLB` e.g. during the boot process.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Security Configuration (`mseccfg`) Register

sentences.text: Executing code with Machine mode privileges is only possible from memory regions with a matching *M-mode-only* rule or a *locked* *Shared-Region* rule with executable privileges.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Security Configuration (`mseccfg`) Register

sentences.text: Executing code from a region without a matching rule or with a matching S/U-mode-only rule is *denied*.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Security Configuration (`mseccfg`) Register

sentences.text: If `mseccfg.MML` is not set, the combination of `pmpcfg.RW=01` remains reserved for future standard use.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Security Configuration (`mseccfg`) Register

sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Security Configuration (`mseccfg`) Register

sentences.text: If Smmpm is not implemented, `PMM` is read-only zero.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Security Configuration (`mseccfg`) Register

sentences.text: The `PMM` field is read-only zero for RV32.
sentences.section: Preamble > Machine-Level ISA, Version 1.13 > Machine-Level CSRs > Machine Security Configuration (`mseccfg`) Register
